arts.ttl: Friday development brings Bournemouth defender close to January move – Exit ‘one step away’
arts.sum: Valencia have identified AFC Bournemouth’s Max Aarons as their main target to strengthen defence in the winter market. On Friday, Super Deporte reported the right-back has attracted interest from the Premier League but wants to join Los Ches. Saturday’s edition of Sport states the La Liga side shifted their focus to the Bournemouth player after The post Friday development brings Bournemouth defender close to January move – Exit ‘one step away’ appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Raphael Wicky is the multi-lingual trophy winner who West Brom want as manager
arts.sum: Raphael Wicky is in line to become West Bromwich Albion's new boss as they close in on the successor to Carlos Corberan ahead of their return to Championship action later this month. Albion have been steered through the festive period by Chris Brunt, Damia Abella and Boaz Myhill, but they will now have their new permanent boss in place by the next Championship game, when they welcome Stoke City to The Hawthorns on the 18th January - although they have the FA Cup tie with Premier League Bournemouth firstly, today, and they'll remain under the guidance of the interim unit.

arts.ttl: Bournemouth player arrives for medical tests with another club – Official announcement on the way
arts.sum: Two days ago, we covered claims from Italy which stated Napoli have agreed a deal with AFC Bournemouth to sign Philip Billing on a loan deal with an option to buy. Gianluca Di Marzio now brings the latest on the midfielder’s exit from the Premier League side in the winter market. arts.ttl: Napoli Onboard Bournemouth Midfielder in Flash Deal
arts.sum: Napoli considered the request by Chelsea for Cesare Casadei excessive and quickly found a different midfielder to replace Fiorentina-bound Michael Folorunsho: Philip Billing from Bournemouth.
